heading: Funding for the DOL Office of Inspector General for oversight of unemployment provisions
label: 9031 Funding for the DOL Office of Inspector General for oversight of unemployment provisions
text: There are appropriated, out of moneys in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, to the Office of the Inspector General of the Department of Labor, $25,000,000 to carry out audits, investigations, and other oversight activities authorized under the Inspector General Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App.) that are related to the provisions of, and amendments made by, this subtitle, to remain available without fiscal year limitation.1See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextThe Inspector General Act of 1978, referred to in text, is , , , which was set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees, and was substantially repealed and restated in chapter 4 (§ 401 et seq.) of Title 5 by , §§ 3(b), 7, , , 4361. For disposition of sections of the Act into chapter 4 of Title 5, see Disposition Table preceding .Pub. L. 95–45292 Stat. 1101Pub. A of , , , known as the Relief for Workers Affected by Coronavirus Act. For complete classification of subtitle A to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub.
